title: Walnut Beet Salad
description: Roasted beets, walnuts, walnut oil, balsamic. Built around a Sunday beet-roasting routine. Holds well and improves after a few hours in the fridge.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Wrap beets individually in foil and roast until a knife slides in easily, about 50–60 minutes. Let cool enough to handle, then rub skins off with a paper towel. Cut into bite-sized wedges or cubes.
While beets roast, toast walnuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 4–5 minutes, stirring frequently, until fragrant. Watch closely — they burn fast. Set aside.
Whisk together walnut oil, balsamic vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a small bowl until emulsified.
Soak red onion in cold water for 10 minutes to mellow the sharpness. Drain and pat dry.
Toss warm or room-temperature beets with the red onion and dressing. Top with toasted walnuts and green onions. Serve immediately or refrigerate — it holds well and improves after a few hours.
notes:
Batch tip: Roast beets on Sunday. Store peeled and cubed in the fridge — they keep 4–5 days. Assembly then takes under 10 minutes any day of the week.

Walnut oil: Use as a finisher only — don't cook with it. Buy small bottles and refrigerate after opening; it goes rancid faster than olive oil.
